rag.description: Export the authenticated user's Google Calendar list during an automation run using token or credentials-based authentication. It applies optional request options — MaxResults (integer 1–250, default 100), MinAccessRole (freeBusyReader, owner, reader, writer), PageToken, presence-activated Switch flags ShowDeleted and ShowHidden, and SyncToken for incremental sync — with the strict constraint that MinAccessRole and SyncToken cannot be combined in the same request. The full CalendarListModel response (ETag, Kind, Items array with id, summary, accessRole and per-entry metadata, NextPageToken, NextSyncToken) is serialized to JSON and stored in ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result so downstream steps can enumerate calendars, page through large lists, or persist NextSyncToken for subsequent incremental syncs without repeating the API call.

qa.question: What does ExportGoogleCalendarList do?
qa.answer: ExportGoogleCalendarList retrieves the authenticated user's list of calendars from the Google Calendar API and stores the full response as JSON in the ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter so later workflow steps can access calendar ids and metadata.

qa.question: What parameters does ExportGoogleCalendarList support?
qa.answer: It supports Credentials and Token for authentication; MaxResults (integer 1–250, default 100) for page size; MinAccessRole (freeBusyReader, owner, reader, writer) for access-role filtering; PageToken for pagination; ShowDeleted and ShowHidden as presence-activated Switch flags to include deleted or hidden calendars; and SyncToken for incremental synchronization. MinAccessRole and SyncToken cannot be used together.

qa.question: How does ExportGoogleCalendarList handle incremental synchronization?
qa.answer: Pass the nextSyncToken value from a previous response as the SyncToken parameter. The API returns only entries changed since that token was issued. Do not combine SyncToken with MinAccessRole. If the token has expired, the API returns 410 GONE and a full re-sync without SyncToken is required.

qa.question: What rule and schema details should I follow to run ExportGoogleCalendarList?
qa.answer: Create an Action rule with pluginName ExportGoogleCalendarList and pass parameters via argument: {{$ --Credentials:my-google-creds --MaxResults:25 --MinAccessRole:writer}}. Presence-only Switch flags use {{$ --ShowDeleted}} or {{$ --ShowHidden}} without a value. The output lands in ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result as a JSON string.

qa.question: What output does ExportGoogleCalendarList produce?
qa.answer: It produces a single session parameter ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result containing the full serialized CalendarListModel JSON, which includes ETag, Kind, Items (array of calendar entries with id, summary, accessRole, and other metadata), NextPageToken, and NextSyncToken.

qa.question: How does ExportGoogleCalendarList handle errors and edge cases?
qa.answer: Workflows should handle invalid or expired tokens, credential resolution failures, insufficient OAuth scopes, network timeouts, non-success API responses, SyncToken expiry (410 GONE requiring full re-sync), invalid MinAccessRole values, MaxResults outside the 1–250 range, expired PageToken, and the unsupported combination of MinAccessRole with SyncToken.

description: ### Purpose
The ExportGoogleCalendarList plugin retrieves the authenticated user's list of calendars from the Google Calendar API.
It helps automation workflows discover, filter, and reference calendars without opening the Google Calendar interface.
The plugin supports authentication using either a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference that is exchanged for a fresh token at runtime.
The exported calendar list is stored as JSON in a session parameter so later workflow steps can safely access calendar ids and metadata.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                     | Description                                                                                    |
| Export Calendar List        | Retrieves all calendars on the authenticated user's calendar list via the Google Calendar API. |
| Access Role Filtering       | Filters results by minimum access role: freeBusyReader, owner, reader, or writer.              |
| Page Size Control           | Limits the number of entries returned per page using MaxResults (1–250, default 100).          |
| Pagination Support          | Accepts a PageToken to retrieve a specific page of results from a previous request.            |
| Show Deleted Entries        | Includes deleted calendar list entries in the response when ShowDeleted is present.            |
| Show Hidden Entries         | Includes hidden calendar list entries in the response when ShowHidden is present.              |
| Incremental Synchronization | Accepts a SyncToken to retrieve only entries that changed since the token was issued.          |
| Token Authentication        | Accepts a direct OAuth Bearer access token for API access.                                     |
| Credentials Authentication  | Uses a stored credential record to generate a fresh access token at runtime.                   |
| Session Result Storage      | Saves the full API response as JSON in the ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter.  |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                      | Description                                                                                       |
| Discover Calendars            | Retrieve all calendars so later steps can locate the correct calendar by id or summary.           |
| Role-Based Calendar Selection | Filter calendars by access role to find only those the user owns or has write access to.          |
| Paged Calendar Export         | Use MaxResults and PageToken to iterate over large calendar lists in controlled pages.            |
| Incremental Calendar Sync     | Pass a stored SyncToken to fetch only calendars changed since the last export, reducing API load. |
| Hidden and Deleted Auditing   | Enable ShowDeleted and ShowHidden to include all entries in auditing or compliance workflows.     |

edge_cases: Credentials value overrides Token when both are provided
Credentials id/name not found
Credentials value is empty or whitespace-only
Credentials record missing refresh token
Refresh-token exchange fails
Google Calendar API returns non-success status code
Google Calendar API returns malformed JSON
Insufficient OAuth scopes for the Google Calendar API
Network timeout or connection failure
No calendars returned (Items array empty or absent)
ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter cannot be written
Token contains leading/trailing whitespace
Token is empty or whitespace-only
Invalid or expired OAuth access token
annotations.expected_result: The full calendar list JSON is stored in the ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter.
annotations.notes: Retrieves the authenticated user's full calendar list using default options (up to 100 entries, no role filter, no pagination) by passing a direct OAuth access token.

description: ### ExportGoogleCalendarList: Export using access token
Retrieve the authenticated user's full Google Calendar list using an OAuth access token with default options.
Store the returned JSON in the ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter.
A macro token produces a runtime token value that is substituted where the token appears, and ExportGoogleCalendarList uses that substituted value to authorize the API request.

edge_cases: Both Token and Credentials provided — Token is ignored
Credentials id/name not found
Credentials record missing refresh token
Refresh-token exchange fails
MaxResults set below 1 or above 250
MinAccessRole set to an unsupported value
MinAccessRole combined with SyncToken — unsupported, API returns error
No calendars match the MinAccessRole filter (Items empty)
Google Calendar API returns non-success status code
Insufficient OAuth scopes for the Google Calendar API
Network timeout or connection failure
ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter cannot be written
annotations.expected_result: At most 25 calendar entries where the user has at least writer access are stored in ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result.
annotations.notes: Retrieves up to 25 calendar entries filtered to calendars where the authenticated user holds at least writer access, using stored credentials for token exchange.

description: ### ExportGoogleCalendarList: Export with access role filter and page size
Retrieve up to 25 Google Calendar entries for which the authenticated user holds at least writer access.
Resolve a stored credentials reference at runtime to obtain an OAuth access token before calling the API.
Store the filtered calendar list JSON in the ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter.

edge_cases: SyncToken expired — API returns 410 GONE; full re-sync required
SyncToken combined with MinAccessRole — unsupported, API returns error
SyncToken used without ShowDeleted or ShowHidden — deleted or hidden changes may be missed
PageToken passed alongside SyncToken — ambiguous pagination state
Credentials id/name not found
Credentials record missing refresh token
Refresh-token exchange fails
Google Calendar API returns non-success status code
No changed entries since SyncToken was issued — Items array is empty
NextSyncToken absent in response — sync state cannot be persisted
Insufficient OAuth scopes for the Google Calendar API
Network timeout or connection failure
ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter cannot be written
annotations.expected_result: Only calendar entries changed since the previous sync token was issued are stored in ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result, including any deleted or hidden entries.
annotations.notes: Performs an incremental sync by supplying a stored SyncToken with ShowDeleted and ShowHidden flags so that removed or hidden calendars are captured in the delta response.

description: ### ExportGoogleCalendarList: Incremental sync with ShowDeleted and ShowHidden
Retrieve only Google Calendar list entries that changed since a previously stored sync token was issued.
Enable ShowDeleted and ShowHidden so deleted and hidden calendar entries are included in the delta response.
Store the incremental result in the ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter and extract NextSyncToken from the response for use in the next sync cycle.
Stores the full Google Calendar list response returned by the Google Calendar API.
Contains a JSON-serialized CalendarListModel including ETag, Kind, Items (calendar entries with id, summary, accessRole, and metadata), NextPageToken, and NextSyncToken.
Allows later workflow steps to extract specific calendar ids, enumerate available calendars, or persist the NextSyncToken for incremental synchronization without repeating the API call.
outputParameters.name: ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result

description: Specifies the identifier or name of a stored credential record used to obtain an OAuth access token at runtime.
Avoids hard-coding short-lived access tokens by exchanging stored credentials for a fresh token when needed.
Takes precedence over the Token parameter when both are provided so authentication remains consistent and renewable.
parameters.name: Credentials

description: Sets the maximum number of calendar list entries returned on a single result page.
Accepts integer values from 1 to 250; the default is 100 when the parameter is not specified.
Helps control API response size and memory usage when a user has many calendars.
parameters.name: MaxResults

description: Filters the calendar list to entries where the authenticated user holds at least the specified access role.
Accepted values are freeBusyReader, owner, reader, and writer.
Cannot be combined with SyncToken; the Google Calendar API will return an error if both parameters are specified.
parameters.name: MinAccessRole

description: Specifies a token returned by a previous calendar list request that identifies the next page of results.
Enables pagination through large calendar lists by continuing from where the previous request left off.
Omit this parameter to start from the first page of results.
parameters.name: PageToken

description: Instructs the plugin to include deleted calendar list entries in the response when present.
Activated by the presence of the parameter key; no value is required.
Useful for auditing workflows that need to detect calendars removed from the user's list, and required alongside ShowHidden when using SyncToken.
parameters.name: ShowDeleted

description: Instructs the plugin to include hidden calendar list entries in the response when present.
Activated by the presence of the parameter key; no value is required.
Required alongside ShowDeleted when using SyncToken so that hidden entries changed since the last sync are captured in the delta.
parameters.name: ShowHidden

description: Provides the nextSyncToken value returned by a previous calendar list response to enable incremental synchronization.
When supplied, the API returns only entries that changed since the token was issued, reducing data volume and API load.
Cannot be combined with MinAccessRole. If the token has expired, the API returns 410 GONE and a full re-sync without SyncToken is required.
parameters.name: SyncToken

description: Supplies an OAuth Bearer access token used to authenticate requests to the Google Calendar API.
Allows direct API access without retrieving credentials from a stored record.
Is ignored when the Credentials parameter is supplied because stored credentials take precedence.
parameters.name: Token

summary: The ExportGoogleCalendarList plugin retrieves the authenticated user's list of calendars from the Google Calendar API.
It supports filtering by minimum access role, controlling page size, paginating through results, including deleted or hidden entries, and performing incremental synchronization via a sync token.

The full API response is serialized to JSON and stored in the ExportGoogleCalendarList:Result session parameter so later workflow steps can access calendar ids and metadata without repeating the API call.
